myths.Agent_1:
Long ago, before the mountains had voices, lived a spirit named Aela. She was the weaver of wind, tasked with carrying the songs of birds across the valleys. Aela loved a mortal shepherd named Kael, who played the most beautiful melodies on his wooden flute. Though she could not speak to him, she danced in the rustling leaves whenever he played.

One autumn, a bitter frost swept the land. Kael, caught in the snow, called out for help, but the heavy snowfall muffled his cries. Desperate, Aela gathered all her strength to catch his voice, weaving it into the very stone of the mountains so it would never be lost. The magic cracked the silent peaks, and for the first time, the mountains answered back, carrying Kael’s cry across the world until the village heard him.

Kael was saved, but Aela had expended her physical form. She dissolved into the air, becoming invisible forever. To this day, when you wander into the deep canyons and call out, it is Aela who catches your words and throws them back, a timeless reminder of the wind spirit who gave the mountains their voice to save the one she loved.

Long before humans walked the earth, the world was cloaked in perpetual twilight. The sky was a canopy of cold, grey clouds, and the land shivered under a blanket of frost. Deep within the highest peak of the jagged Iron Mountains lived Ignis, a solitary bird with feathers of dull charcoal. Ignis possessed a secret: a burning heart that glowed with an agonizing, restless heat.

One day, the cold became so unbearable that the great ancient trees began to weep sap that froze instantly into amber tears. Moved by the sorrow of the forest, Ignis took flight. He soared higher than any creature had ever dared, piercing the thick, grey clouds to reach the hidden realm of the Sun.

Bathing in the forbidden light, Ignis’s charcoal feathers ignited into brilliant shades of crimson and gold. He became a living meteor, plunging back down to the frozen earth. Wherever he landed, his fiery plumage set the dry brush ablaze, gifting the shivering world its first warmth.

But the fire consumed his physical form. Ignis vanished, leaving behind only glowing embers. Today, whenever a spark catches and a hearth crackles to life, it is the spirit of Ignis, still warming the world.

Myth: Long before the oceans had tides, the northern lands were trapped in an endless, suffocating night. The sky was an empty void of black glass, and the creatures below huddled in fear of the creeping shadows. Deep within the frozen tundra lived Lira, a majestic wolf with a coat of pale starlight and eyes that held the quiet glow of the moon. Lira possessed a secret: her spirit was bound to the cosmic winds, restless and bright.

One winter, the darkness grew so heavy that it began to smother the very breath of the world. Moved by the despair of the frozen earth, Lira began to run. She sprinted up the highest glacier, leaping into the black void of the sky. As her paws struck the empty heavens, she ignited trails of shimmering emerald, violet, and sapphire light. She ran tirelessly, weaving a tapestry of brilliant colors to chase the shadows away.

But the celestial run consumed her mortal body. Lira dissolved into ribbons of pure light, leaving the earth behind forever. Today, when the northern skies dance with breathtaking colors, it is Lira’s spirit still running across the heavens, guarding the world from the dark.
